summary: Validated legacy nonpaged-pool size control. Microsoft Q&A captures NonPagedPoolSize = 0 as the default value, and the app writes that same reset value.

setting.name: Nonpaged Pool Size
setting.casual_explanation: Nonpaged pool is kernel memory that must always stay resident in RAM. This is a low-level legacy tuning area that normal users should not adjust manually.
setting.why_people_change_it: People only touch it when undoing old memory tweaks or investigating unusual kernel memory issues on older systems.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The current app writes the documented default reset value, but this remains a low-level legacy tuning area.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Q&A now captures NonPagedPoolSize = 0 as part of the default Memory Management registry snapshot. The current app writes NonPagedPoolSize = 0 under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management, matching that default reset value.

allowed_values.label: Default nonpaged pool size
allowed_values.meaning: The default reset value captured in Microsoft Q&A.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NonPagedPoolSize = 0, matching the default value captured in Microsoft Q&A.

writes.notes: Observed app value now matches the documented default.

windows_defaults.label: Conservative publication baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Modern systems where this dataset has not yet published a validated NonPagedPoolSize reset mapping

states.rationale: Microsoft Q&A captures NonPagedPoolSize = 0 as the default value.

windows_defaults.notes: This record treats the default reset value as validated from Microsoft Q&A and documents the legacy tuning as low-level research, not a modern performance tweak.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: Research comparisons against the current app write

states.rationale: Keep the documented default reset value.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the documented default nonpaged-pool behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are trying to document the current app implementation as a non-default tweak.
tradeoffs: The current app write matches the documented default reset.

evidence.title: Microsoft Q&A: default registry value for Memory Management
evidence.summary: Microsoft Q&A captures NonPagedPoolSize = 0 as part of the default Memory Management registry snapshot.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Memory Management Registry Keys
evidence.summary: Microsoft's current published memory-management article documents NonPagedPoolLimit for 32-bit systems, but not NonPagedPoolSize as a current modern limit surface.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Memory Limits for Windows Releases
evidence.summary: Microsoft's memory-limits documentation discusses nonpaged-pool limits and notes that newer Windows versions use NonPagedPoolLimit, reinforcing that the currently published surface differs from the app's NonPagedPoolSize write.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NonPagedPoolSize = 0 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft Q&A now captures NonPagedPoolSize = 0 as part of the default Memory Management registry snapshot. The record matches the app and is validated for research, but it remains a low-level legacy tuning rather than a general Windows performance tweak.

validation_proof.notes: Microsoft Q&A captures the default Memory Management registry snapshot and includes NonPagedPoolSize = 0.
